forecaster_note: Fun, easing surf with offshore winds — get it early for the most size and the dropping tide. SE/ESE swell still offering head-high sets at the better exposures, smaller elsewhere. Winds stay favourable through midday before a light northerly drift — not enough to ruin it, but quality holds better at S-facing breaks. Sun: small but worth a paddle with all-day offshores, crisp and clean. INCOMING: next SE–E swell fills in Wednesday for another round of decent to maybe solid size. — Kurt Korte (Surfline)

wind.summary: N–NNE 8kph (13–14 gusts) — the forecast light northerly drift has arrived. Offshore at Long Reef, light cross at DY/Curly, gentle onshore at Manly. Still very surfable.

shark.summary: Two Northern Beaches alerts in 24h: 2.94m White Shark tagged & released from the Dee Why SMART drumline Fri 4:21pm, and a shark spotted by SLS drone at Long Reef 10:32am today — water was evacuated. Stay sharp at DY/Long Reef, surf with company.

days.label: Today
days.note: Arvo update: SE/ESE swell easing but still fun — ride the push into the 5:36pm high before last light 5:29pm. Light northerly drift, cleanest at S-facing breaks. 🦈 Shark activity at Dee Why (drumline tag Fri arvo) and Long Reef (drone sighting this morning, water evacuated) — buddy up.

dee-why.window: Swell fades through the arvo — get in on the incoming ahead of the 5:36pm high. N breeze is a light cross, still clean enough.
alerts: 🦈 2.94m White Shark tagged & released from the DY SMART drumline Fri 4:21pm — stay alert

tip.label: Point call
tip.text: 171kJ at 66/100 — fun chest–head walls. SE 139° + ESE 111° is a good angle here (dead-S is only 7kJ background, no bad wrap). Frontside rights at the Point on the incoming tide.

long-reef.window: Standout of the four this arvo — 197kJ, NNE offshore and chest–overhead sets on the reefs. Incoming tide to 5:36pm suits it.
alerts: 🦈 Shark spotted by SLS drone 10:32am today — water was evacuated. Check conditions before paddling out

tip.label: Best bet
tip.text: Best rating (fair–good) and most energy on the stretch, with genuine offshore. Weigh it against this morning's drone sighting — go with a mate.

curl-curl.window: Clean-enough beachie peaks on the incoming — the S-swell magnet holds size as the SE fades. Light cross only.

tip.label: Plan B
tip.text: 167kJ at 67/100 — most consistent of the four. Backside lefts or frontside rights depending on the bank; no shark flags here today.

manly.window: Smallest and least clean today — light NNE onshore ruffles it. Only worth it if you're already there.

tip.label: Skip
tip.text: Waist–shoulder with a gentle onshore — DY, Long Reef and Curly all beat it this arvo.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: Small but worth a paddle — all-day offshores, crisp and clean. Groveler/fish day. The 10–12 slot rides the drop to the 11:50am low: fun, uncrowded peelers.

dee-why.window: Small, crisp and uncrowded — bring volume and pick the low-tide banks around midday.

long-reef.window: Best size-holder Sunday — the reefs sweep up the leftover ESE energy. Mid-morning on the dropping tide.

curl-curl.window: Clean little beachie peaks all day — groveler territory at the better banks.

manly.window: Tiny but tidy — longboard cruise or skip.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: Sat delivered early and is fading into the evening high. Sun: 1–3ft leftovers with all-day offshores — small, crisp fish/longboard territory. Next real SE–E swell fills in Wednesday (Long Reef showing 6–8ft on the charts — watch it).

dee-why.window: Sat's session window has largely passed; Sun is small, clean and empty — volume required.
alerts: 🦈 White Shark tagged at the DY drumline Fri arvo — stay aware through the weekend

long-reef.window: Sunday's best size on the stretch — reefs hold the leftover ESE pulse.
alerts: 🦈 Drone sighting Sat morning (water evacuated) — check SharkSmart before Sunday's paddle

curl-curl.window: Small clean peaks Sunday — fun on a groveler at the banks.

manly.window: Tiny Sunday — log it or leave it.
